hints.to save: 'to save' is 'salvare' — 'salve' comes from 'salvere' (to be well/hail)

hints.Indicative: Indicative of 'salvere' would be 'salves' — 'salve' is the imperative greeting form
hints.Subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'salveas' — 'salve' is the imperative form meaning 'hail!'
hints.Infinitive: Infinitive would be 'salvere' — 'salve' is the imperative singular

hints.salvo, salvare, salvavi, salvatum: These are the principal parts of 'salvare' (to save)
hints.saluto, salutare, salutavi, salutatum: These are the principal parts of 'salutare' (to greet)
hints.sano, sanare, sanavi, sanatum: These are the principal parts of 'sanare' (to heal)

hints.Nominative: While 'Regina' has the same form in nominative and vocative for 1st decl., here it is addressed directly
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'Regina' would be 'Reginae' — 'Regina' is the vocative form of address
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'Regina' would be 'Reginam' — 'Regina' is vocative

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'reginae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'reginae' ends in -ae
hints.5th declension: 5th declension nouns have genitive in -ei — 'reginae' ends in -ae

hints.Nominative: While the form is the same, 'mater' is in apposition to 'Regina' as a vocative address
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'mater' would be 'matris' — 'mater' here is vocative
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'mater' would be 'matrem' — 'mater' here is vocative

hints.Nominative: Nominative singular is 'misericordia' — 'misericordiae' with -ae is genitive singular here
hints.Dative: While 'misericordiae' could be dative, here it is genitive modifying 'mater'
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'misericordia' would be 'misericordia' — 'misericordiae' is genitive

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'misericordiae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'misericordiae' ends in -ae
hints.5th declension: 5th declension nouns have genitive in -ei — 'misericordiae' ends in -ae
